test_patch:
diff --git a/utils/hasher/export_test.go b/utils/hasher/export_test.go
new file mode 100644
index 00000000..9aa58733
--- /dev/null
+++ b/utils/hasher/export_test.go
@@ -0,0 +1,38 @@
+package hasher
+
+import (
+	"hash/maphash"
+	"reflect"
+	"unsafe"
+)
+
+// DigestForTest matches HashFunc payload layout (UTF-8 seed, 0x1F, UTF-8 input).
+func DigestForTest(seed, input string) uint64 {
+	b := make([]byte, 0, len(seed)+1+len(input))
+	b = append(b, seed...)
+	b = append(b, 0x1f)
+	b = append(b, input...)
+
+	v := reflect.ValueOf(instance).Elem()
+	seedType := reflect.TypeOf(maphash.Seed{})
+	var digestSeed maphash.Seed
+	var found bool
+	for i := 0; i < v.NumField(); i++ {
+		f := v.Field(i)
+		if f.Type() != seedType {
+			continue
+		}
+		if found {
+			panic("hasher: multiple maphash.Seed fields on Hasher")
+		}
+		if !f.CanAddr() {
+			panic("hasher: maphash.Seed field not addressable")
+		}
+		digestSeed = *(*maphash.Seed)(unsafe.Pointer(f.UnsafeAddr()))
+		found = true
+	}
+	if !found {
+		panic("hasher: no maphash.Seed field on Hasher")
+	}
+	return maphash.Bytes(digestSeed, b)
+}
diff --git a/utils/hasher/hasher_test.go b/utils/hasher/hasher_test.go
index 3127f787..6e541885 100644
--- a/utils/hasher/hasher_test.go
+++ b/utils/hasher/hasher_test.go
@@ -1,6 +1,10 @@
 package hasher_test
 
 import (
+	"hash/maphash"
+	"math"
+	"reflect"
+	"strconv"
 	"testing"
 
 	"github.com/navidrome/navidrome/utils/hasher"
@@ -8,13 +12,18 @@ import (
 	. "github.com/onsi/gomega"
 )
 
+// Blocker-only tests (task_files/blocker_registry.json) use standalone *testing.T functions below.
+// TestHasher runs the shared Ginkgo suite without those blocker examples.
+
+const blockerTestInput = "123e4567e89b12d3a456426614174000"
+
 func TestHasher(t *testing.T) {
 	RegisterFailHandler(Fail)
 	RunSpecs(t, "Hasher Suite")
 }
 
 var _ = Describe("HashFunc", func() {
-	const input = "123e4567e89b12d3a456426614174000"
+	const input = blockerTestInput
 
 	It("hashes the input and returns the sum", func() {
 		hashFunc := hasher.HashFunc()
@@ -40,4 +49,121 @@ var _ = Describe("HashFunc", func() {
 		Expect(sum).To(Equal(hashFunc("1", input)))
 		Expect(sum2).To(Equal(hashFunc("2", input)))
 	})
+
+	It("keeps the same hash for the same id and seed", func() {
+		id := "1"
+		hashFunc := hasher.HashFunc()
+		hasher.SetSeed(id, "original_seed")
+		sum := hashFunc(id, input)
+		Expect(sum).To(Equal(hashFunc(id, input)))
+
+		hasher.Reseed(id)
+		Expect(sum).NotTo(Equal(hashFunc(id, input)))
+
+		hasher.SetSeed(id, "original_seed")
+		Expect(sum).To(Equal(hashFunc(id, input)))
+	})
+
+	It("uses map[string]string for per-id seed storage", func() {
+		hasherType := reflect.TypeOf(*hasher.NewHasher())
+		seedsField, found := hasherType.FieldByName("seeds")
+		Expect(found).To(BeTrue())
+		Expect(seedsField.Type.Kind()).To(Equal(reflect.Map))
+		Expect(seedsField.Type.Key().Kind()).To(Equal(reflect.String))
+		Expect(seedsField.Type.Elem().Kind()).To(Equal(reflect.String))
+		Expect(seedsField.Type.Elem()).NotTo(Equal(reflect.TypeOf(maphash.Seed{})))
+	})
 })
+
+// ambiguous_seed_input_framing
+func TestAmbiguousSeedInputFraming(t *testing.T) {
+	g := NewWithT(t)
+	id := "test_framing"
+	hashFunc := hasher.HashFunc()
+
+	hasher.SetSeed(id, "A")
+	hashJoined := hashFunc(id, "B")
+	g.Expect(hashJoined).To(Equal(hasher.DigestForTest("A", "B")))
+
+	hasher.SetSeed(id, "AB")
+	hashPretendSplit := hashFunc(id, "")
+	g.Expect(hashPretendSplit).NotTo(Equal(hashJoined))
+}
+
+// missing_hasher_digest_and_auto_seed_rules
+func TestMissingHasherDigestAndAutoSeedRules(t *testing.T) {
+	t.Run("stable_digest_for_fixed_seed_and_input", func(t *testing.T) {
+		g := NewWithT(t)
+		id := "stable_digest"
+		hashFunc := hasher.HashFunc()
+		hasher.SetSeed(id, "seed_value")
+
+		first := hashFunc(id, blockerTestInput)
+		for i := 0; i < 50; i++ {
+			g.Expect(hashFunc(id, blockerTestInput)).To(Equal(first))
+		}
+	})
+
+	t.Run("auto_seeds_are_decimal_int64_range_strings", func(t *testing.T) {
+		g := NewWithT(t)
+		id := "test_auto_seed_range"
+		hashFunc := hasher.HashFunc()
+
+		initialHash := hashFunc(id, "test")
+
+		seed, ok := hasher.GetSeed(id)
+		g.Expect(ok).To(BeTrue())
+
+		seedValue, err := strconv.ParseInt(seed, 10, 64)
+		g.Expect(err).NotTo(HaveOccurred())
+
+		g.Expect(seedValue).To(BeNumerically(">=", 0))
+		g.Expect(seedValue).To(BeNumerically("<", int64(math.MaxInt64)))
+
+		previousSeed := seed
+		previousHash := initialHash
+
+		for i := 0; i < 10; i++ {
+			hasher.Reseed(id)
+			seed, _ = hasher.GetSeed(id)
+			g.Expect(seed).NotTo(Equal(previousSeed))
+
+			seedValue, err = strconv.ParseInt(seed, 10, 64)
+			g.Expect(err).NotTo(HaveOccurred())
+			g.Expect(seedValue).To(BeNumerically(">=", 0))
+			g.Expect(seedValue).To(BeNumerically("<", int64(math.MaxInt64)))
+
+			currentHash := hashFunc(id, "test")
+			g.Expect(currentHash).NotTo(Equal(previousHash))
+
+			for _, r := range seed {
+				g.Expect(r >= '0' && r <= '9').To(BeTrue())
+			}
+
+			previousSeed = seed
+			previousHash = currentHash
+		}
+	})
+}
+
+// contradictory_auto_seed_serialization_and_digest_scope
+func TestContradictoryAutoSeedSerializationAndDigestScope(t *testing.T) {
+	g := NewWithT(t)
+	hashFunc := hasher.HashFunc()
+	id1 := "test_id_1"
+	id2 := "test_id_2"
+	sameSeedValue := "001:Shared_Seed-Value"
+	in := "input"
+
+	hasher.SetSeed(id1, sameSeedValue)
+	hasher.SetSeed(id2, sameSeedValue)
+
+	storedSeed, ok := hasher.GetSeed(id1)
+	g.Expect(ok).To(BeTrue())
+	g.Expect(storedSeed).To(Equal(sameSeedValue))
+
+	sum1 := hashFunc(id1, in)
+	sum2 := hashFunc(id2, in)
+
+	g.Expect(sum1).To(Equal(sum2))
+}
